wikipedia_title: Kerala school of astronomy and mathematics
display_title: Kerala school of astronomy and mathematics

annotations.label: Infinite geometric series

anchor.snippet: These include the following infinite geometric series

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the geometric series sum ∑ rⁿ = (1-r)⁻¹ for 0 ≤ r < 1 (and the normed generalization tsum_geometric_of_norm_lt_one).

annotations.label: Sum of integral powers for large n

anchor.snippet: They used this to discover a semi-rigorous proof of the result

annotations.note: Faulhaber's theorem (sum_range_pow) gives the exact closed form for ∑ iᵖ whose leading term is nᵖ⁺¹/(p+1), but the asymptotic limit statement itself is not separately formalized.

annotations.label: Taylor–Maclaurin series for sine, cosine, and arctangent

anchor.snippet: The Tantrasangraha-vakhya gives the series in verse

annotations.note: Real.hasSum_sin, Real.hasSum_cos, and Real.hasSum_arctan give the Maclaurin series for sine, cosine, and arctangent respectively.

annotations.label: Standard power series for trigonometric functions

anchor.snippet: the series reduce to the standard power series for these trigonometric functions, for example

annotations.note: The standard power series for sin and cos are formalized as Real.hasSum_sin/Real.hasSum_cos (and the complex versions Complex.hasSum_sin/cos).

annotations.label: Gregory series for π

anchor.snippet: an infinite series expression (later known as Gregory series)

annotations.note: Real.tendsto_sum_pi_div_four proves the Gregory–Leibniz series 1 - 1/3 + 1/5 - ... = π/4.

annotations.label: Error estimate for finite partial sums

anchor.snippet: Their rational approximation of the error for the finite sum of their series are of particular interest

annotations.note: Mathlib's alternating_series_error_bound gives the standard |tail| ≤ next-term estimate, but the Kerala-style refined rational correction terms for the error are not formalized.

annotations.label: Accelerated series for π via partial fractions

anchor.snippet: to obtain a more rapidly converging series for

annotations.note: No Mathlib declaration formalizes the partial-fraction-accelerated convergent series for π.

annotations.label: Rational approximation of π to nine decimal places

anchor.snippet: They used the improved series to derive a rational expression

annotations.note: No Mathlib declaration provides this specific rational approximation of π accurate to nine decimal places.

annotations.label: Differentiation of trigonometric functions

anchor.snippet: also gave a semi-rigorous method of differentiation of some trigonometric functions

annotations.note: Derivatives of trig functions are formalized, e.g. Real.deriv_sin (= cos) and Real.deriv_cos (= -sin), with HasDerivAt forms hasDerivAt_sin/hasDerivAt_cos.
